note: Group exposes two content entities (group, group_relationship), three config entities (group_type, group_role, group_relationship_type), the GroupRelationType plugin type, and per-group permissions via flexible_permissions. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using the Group module, which of these is a content entity you create at runtime and which is a config entity (bundle): `group` vs `group_type`? What about `group_role`?
evals.expected_output: `group` is a content entity (an actual group instance); `group_type` is its config-entity bundle; `group_role` is also a config entity (a role scoped to a group type). `group_relationship` is a content entity, `group_relationship_type` is config.

evals.prompt: In Group v3 on Drupal 11, I want to make a custom entity type relatable to groups. Which plugin type do I implement, which attribute/namespace, and which base class?
evals.expected_output: Implement a GroupRelationType plugin: class in the Plugin\Group\Relation namespace, annotated with the #[GroupRelationType] attribute, extending GroupRelationBase. The manager is group_relation_type.manager.

evals.prompt: I'm upgrading a module from Group v2 to v3. What did the GroupContent entity, GroupContentType, and the GroupContentEnabler plugin type get renamed to?
evals.expected_output: GroupContent -> GroupRelationship (group_relationship), GroupContentType -> GroupRelationshipType (group_relationship_type), and the GroupContentEnabler plugin type -> GroupRelationType.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, how do I add a user as a member of a group, and how do I relate an arbitrary entity to a group? Show the group entity methods.
evals.expected_output: $group->addMember($user) adds a member; $group->addRelationship($entity, $plugin_id) relates any entity. Both create group_relationship records.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 Group v3 site, how should I load all memberships of a user in code? Note: the old membership loader service is deprecated.
evals.expected_output: Use the static methods on Drupal\group\Entity\GroupMembership: loadByUser($account), loadByGroup($group), loadSingle($group, $account). The group.membership_loader service (GroupMembershipLoaderInterface) is deprecated since 3.2.0.

evals.prompt: How does Group grant per-group permissions on Drupal 11, and what are the three group role scopes? Which module actually calculates the permissions?
evals.expected_output: Per-group permissions are granted by group_role config entities with scope outsider, insider or individual (outsider/insider synchronize with a global role). Calculation is delegated to the flexible_permissions module via group_permission.calculator/checker.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 Group site, how do I check whether a user has a particular permission inside a specific group (not sitewide)? Give the entity method and the underlying service.
evals.expected_output: $group->hasPermission($permission, $account); it delegates to the group_permission.checker service (GroupPermissionCheckerInterface::hasPermissionInGroup).

evals.prompt: Which Group submodule lets me relate core nodes to groups, and which submodule makes revision access over grouped entities depend on the group?
evals.expected_output: gnode (Group Node) relates nodes to groups via the group_node relation plugin; group_support_revisions makes per-group revision access possible by decorating the permission providers.
